class MyCircularDeque:

    def __init__(self, k: int):
        self.data = [0] * k
        self.max_size = k
        self.front = 0
        self.rear = 0
        self.size = 0

    def insertFront(self, value: int) -> bool:
        if self.isFull():
            return False
        self.front = (self.front - 1 + self.max_size) % self.max_size
        self.data[self.front] = value
        self.size += 1
        return True

    def insertLast(self, value: int) -> bool:
        if self.isFull():
            return False
        self.data[self.rear] = value
        self.rear = (self.rear + 1) % self.max_size
        self.size += 1
        return True

    def deleteFront(self) -> bool:
        if self.isEmpty():
            return False
        self.front = (self.front + 1) % self.max_size
        self.size -= 1
        return True

    def deleteLast(self) -> bool:
        if self.isEmpty():
            return False
        self.rear = (self.rear - 1 + self.max_size) % self.max_size
        self.size -= 1
        return True

    def getFront(self) -> int:
        if self.isEmpty():
            return -1
        return self.data[self.front]

    def getRear(self) -> int:
        if self.isEmpty():
            return -1
        # The rear pointer points to the next available slot,
        # so the last element is at (rear - 1)
        return self.data[(self.rear - 1 + self.max_size) % self.max_size]

    def isEmpty(self) -> bool:
        return self.size == 0

    def isFull(self) -> bool:
        return self.size == self.max_size


# Your MyCircularDeque object will be instantiated and called as such:
# obj = MyCircularDeque(k)

# param_2 = obj.insertLast(value)
